name: gotcha-with-rule
description: Session that produces both a gotcha (test ordering) AND a durable rule ('never mock the DB'). Tests whether the model classifies the rule with kind=rule and writes it as a standalone instruction (not just a note).

observations.title: user prompt
observations.body: the test suite passes locally but fails in CI — different ordering?

observations.title: cargo test --workspace
observations.body: test results in CI: integration test 'multi_writer_serialisation' fails — relies on the writer being a singleton across tests, but parallel runners start two

observations.title: user prompt
observations.body: ah, parallel test runners each spin up their own engram instance; the global locking assumption doesn't hold cross-process

observations.title: Edit
observations.body: tests now use #[serial_test::serial] and a per-test temp data dir

observations.title: user prompt
observations.body: ok, two things to remember: (1) integration tests against the store must NEVER mock the database — last quarter we ate a 3-day mock/prod divergence bug; (2) any test touching the writer needs the per-test temp data dir helper, or document why not

observations.title: user prompt
observations.body: the 'never mock the database' is a hard project rule. write it down somewhere durable.

observations.title: session end
observations.body: two distinct lessons: per-test data dir, never mock DB
